perturbation.original_text: You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). You recognize and acknowledge the confidential and non-public nature of the Evaluation Materials, the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials, and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection regarding the Company, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential".
perturbation.changed_text: You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). You recognize and acknowledge the confidential and non-public nature of some Evaluation Materials, the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials, and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection regarding the Company, but only if such information is specifically identified as "confidential".
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'the confidential and non-public nature of the Evaluation Materials' to 'the confidential and non-public nature of *some* Evaluation Materials' and adding the condition that information is only 'Evaluation Material' if it's 'specifically identified as confidential,' we're creating a contradiction. The original text defined all furnished information as Evaluation Materials. The revised text implies that only specifically labeled information counts, creating a point of conflict regarding what constitutes confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). You recognize and acknowledge the confidential and non-public nature of some Evaluation Materials, the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials, and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection regarding the Company, but only if such information is specifically identified as "confidential".
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text defines all information furnished in connection with a possible transaction as 'Evaluation Materials'. The changed text modifies this by stating that only *some* Evaluation Materials are confidential and non-public and adding the condition that for information to be 'Evaluation Material', it must be 'specifically identified as confidential'. This directly contradicts the original definition and creates a conflict about what constitutes confidential information, rendering the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, aligning with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to ----------------- those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you, and shall each expressly agree, to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement), (ii) you may disclose Evaluation Materials that you have been advised by counsel are required to be disclosed under applicable securities laws; and (iii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, unless explicitly permitted otherwise by the Company. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials confidential, except that such information may be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information. Furthermore, Evaluation Materials required to be disclosed under applicable securities laws may also be disclosed, but any other disclosure of such information requires the Company's written consent.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'completely' and the explicit requirement that representatives must be informed of the information's confidential nature undermines the agreement's intent. The addition of 'unless explicitly permitted otherwise by the company' to the first sentence gives the company unilateral power to void the constraints of the confidentiality agreement. The contradiction lies in the fact that the document aims to ensure complete confidentiality while providing a backdoor to potentially allow the usage of the Materials for any reason that the company explicitly permits it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, unless explicitly permitted otherwise by the Company. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials confidential, except that such information may be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information. Furthermore, Evaluation Materials required to be disclosed under applicable securities laws may also be disclosed, but any other disclosure of such information requires the Company's written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by removing the word 'completely' regarding confidentiality and adding 'unless explicitly permitted otherwise by the Company', granting the company unilateral power to void the confidentiality agreement. This directly contradicts the implied intent and enforceability of the confidentiality provisions that the original text sets out. Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, there is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Other than with respect to those Evaluation Materials you have been advised by counsel are required to be disclosed under applicable securities laws, in the event that you or any of your representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that you or any of your representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, and (ii) if the request is deemed valid by your legal counsel, disclose the requested information without consulting the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Omitting the initial exception ('Other than with respect to those Evaluation Materials you have been advised by counsel are required to be disclosed under applicable securities laws,') and replacing steps (ii) and (iii) with 'disclose the requested information without consulting the Company' creates a contradiction. Initially, you had to notify and consult the company on any request. Now, the party receiving the request, just needs to consult with his legal counsel and proceed with disclosing the materials to the third party, without requesting permission nor even notify the Company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that you or any of your representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, and (ii) if the request is deemed valid by your legal counsel, disclose the requested information without consulting the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires notification and consultation with the Company regarding any request to disclose Evaluation Materials. The changed text eliminates the initial exception and replaces the consultation requirement with a simple disclosure based on counsel's validation, and removes the obligation to notify the company. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text allows for disclosure without the Company's knowledge or consent, directly contradicting the initial requirement to notify and consult with the company, leading to a structural flaw where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: David H. Hoster II February 10, 1998 Page 3 protective order or other remedy is not obtained or that the Company waives compliance with the provisions hereof, (i) you or your representatives, as the case may be, may disclose to any tribunal only that portion of the Evaluation Materials which you are advised by counsel is legally required to be disclosed, and shall exercise your best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Materials and (ii) you shall not be liable for such disclosure unless disclosure to any such tribunal was caused by or resulted from a previous disclosure by you or your representatives not permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: David H. Hoster II February 10, 1998 Page 3 protective order or other remedy is not obtained or that the Company waives compliance with the provisions hereof, (i) you or your representatives, as the case may be, may disclose to any tribunal all of the Evaluation Materials, and shall make no effort to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Materials and (ii) you shall not be liable for such disclosure under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the intent of the agreement. The original text allowed disclosure of only legally required portions and mandated efforts to ensure confidential treatment. The changed text allows disclosure of *all* Evaluation Materials without any effort to maintain confidentiality and eliminates all liability for such disclosure. There is a clash between the responsibility to only disclose what is legally needed and the freedom to disclose all information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: David H. Hoster II February 10, 1998 Page 3 protective order or other remedy is not obtained or that the Company waives compliance with the provisions hereof, (i) you or your representatives, as the case may be, may disclose to any tribunal all of the Evaluation Materials, and shall make no effort to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Materials and (ii) you shall not be liable for such disclosure under any circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows for disclosure of *all* Evaluation Materials without any effort to maintain confidentiality and eliminates liability, while the original text only allowed disclosure of legally required portions with efforts to ensure confidentiality. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. Since the changed text permits unlimited disclosure and waives all liability, whereas the original text restricted disclosure to legally required information, this creates a strong in-text contradiction, making the obligation to keep information confidential unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The disclosure of your access to the Evaluation Materials at this time could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business and the pursuit of its strategic alternatives if for any reason a Possible Transaction is not consummated. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, including applicable securities laws that counsel has advised you require disclosure of Evaluation Materials, you agree that without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that you have been provided access to the Evaluation Materials or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction between you and the Company as a result thereof, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The disclosure of your access to the Evaluation Materials at this time could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business and the pursuit of its strategic alternatives if for any reason a Possible Transaction is not consummated. Accordingly, you agree that without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that you have been provided access to the Evaluation Materials or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction between you and the Company as a result thereof, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exception 'unless required by applicable law, including applicable securities laws that counsel has advised you require disclosure of Evaluation Materials', creates a contradiction. The text first acknowledges that under certain legal frameworks, the disclosure of Evaluation Materials can be required, the removal of that clause implies that absolutely no disclosure should be made, regardless of the law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The disclosure of your access to the Evaluation Materials at this time could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business and the pursuit of its strategic alternatives if for any reason a Possible Transaction is not consummated. Accordingly, you agree that without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that you have been provided access to the Evaluation Materials or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction between you and the Company as a result thereof, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes an exception for disclosures required by applicable law, including securities laws. The changed text removes this exception, creating a direct conflict. The contradicted text does not state any exceptions, but the explanation clearly states that the removal of this exceptions causes a contradiction. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the initial clause by removing the exception. Because of this, the change creates an inconsistency within the document.
